blurb_en:
John Jumper, who shared the 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry for AlphaFold's protein-structure predictions, announced June 19 he is leaving Google DeepMind for Anthropic after nearly nine years — the same week transformer co-author Noam Shazeer departed the same lab for OpenAI.

- AlphaFold 2 predicted the 3D structure of almost every known protein; work scientists estimated would have taken decades otherwise
- Jumper's hire signals Anthropic's push into computational biology and life sciences, not just language and code
- DeepMind has now lost two landmark researchers in one week to its two main rivals
- Anthropic is simultaneously banned from operating in South Korea yet is attracting Nobel-level talent
- Pattern: frontier researchers are choosing safety-focused or independent labs over Google
blurb_zh:
AlphaFold蛋白质预测模型的2024年诺贝尔化学奖得主John Jumper于6月19日宣布，在效力谷歌DeepMind近九年后转投Anthropic。同一周，Transformer论文共同作者Noam Shazeer也离开DeepMind，加盟OpenAI。

- AlphaFold 2在数月内预测出几乎所有已知蛋白质的三维结构，原本可能需要数十年才能完成
- Anthropic此举表明其正将布局延伸至计算生物学与生命科学领域
- DeepMind在一周内分别向Anthropic和OpenAI各失去一位顶级研究员
verification_notes: Confirmed via Bloomberg (June 19), TechCrunch (June 20), The Next Web, TechTimes. Jumper's 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry shared with Hassabis is on record. Shazeer departure confirmed in prior run (2026-06-19-noam-shazeer-google-openai-hire).

headline_zh: 首尔开张，韩国用户仍被封禁
blurb_en:
Anthropic opened its Seoul office on June 17 — its third in Asia-Pacific — on the same day Fable 5 and Mythos 5 remained blocked to every Korean user under a US export-control directive.

- On June 12, the US government barred foreign nationals from accessing Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security
- The White House linked the trigger to SK Telecom — a Korean carrier with alleged historical ties to Chinese state entities — having accessed Mythos 5 through Anthropic's Glasswing program; SK Telecom denies any such ties
- Anthropic disabled both models globally, since selective geographic enforcement on shared cloud infrastructure is impractical
- At the Seoul launch, Anthropic's international head said he was 'very confident' access would return 'in coming days' — no confirmed date as of June 21
- OpenAI, which signed defense contracts with the Trump administration, faces no comparable export restrictions
blurb_zh:
Anthropic于6月17日在首尔开设亚太地区第三个办公室，而同一天，其旗舰模型Fable 5和Mythos 5仍因美国出口管制令对韩国用户封闭。

- 美国政府6月12日发布指令，以国家安全为由限制外国人访问Anthropic最先进模型
- 据报道，SK电信通过Anthropic的Glasswing安全项目获得了Mythos 5访问权限；SK电信否认与中国存在关联
- Anthropic因无法精准实施地区屏蔽，被迫在全球范围内下线两款模型
- 与特朗普政府签有国防合同的OpenAI，则未受类似限制
verification_notes: Seoul office confirmed via Anthropic official page. Export ban context confirmed via Korea JoongAng Daily, TechTimes, CNBC (June 17 'Anthropic asked for regulation'), Al Jazeera. SK Telecom identified by Wired. Substantively different angle from June 12 shutdown story (2026-06-13-anthropic-fable5-mythos5-government-shutdown).

headline_zh: 美国称ASML芯片流入中国 荷兰厂商否认
blurb_en:
US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick told ASML executives June 19 that the government believes at least one extreme ultraviolet lithography machine has made it into China — violating export controls in place since 2019. ASML says no EUV machine has ever been shipped to China.

- EUV machines are the world's most complex manufactured objects; only ASML makes them, and they require dozens of trucks to ship, years to install, and regular ASML service visits
- China has been barred from buying EUV machines since 2019; without them, Chinese fabs cannot produce cutting-edge chips
- ASML tracked all 314 of its EUV systems worldwide and presented documentation showing none in China; the US declined to share its evidence
- If true, it would be the most significant export-control breach in semiconductor history
- EUV access would compress China's chip manufacturing gap with the US by years — directly affecting compute available to Chinese AI labs
blurb_zh:
美国商务部长6月19日向荷兰芯片设备商ASML通报，称相信至少一台极紫外光刻机已流入中国，违反2019年起实施的出口管制规定。ASML否认称，已追踪全球314台EUV设备，无一在中国境内。

- 极紫外光刻机是当今世界上最复杂的制造设备，全球仅ASML生产，出货需数十辆卡车，安装耗时数年
- 中国自2019年起被禁止购买EUV设备，没有它就无法生产先进芯片
- ASML出具内部追踪文件反驳；美国拒绝提供证据
- 若属实，将是半导体史上最严重的出口管制违规事件，中国AI算力格局也将随之改变
verification_notes: Bloomberg broke the story June 19; TechCrunch and Tom's Hardware confirmed. ASML's denial covered by multiple outlets. US government has not presented evidence publicly. The underlying claim is unverified but the reporting from Bloomberg/TC is solid.

headline_zh: 快手Kling AI寻求20亿美元融资 同日推出Turbo版本
blurb_en:
Kuaishou is seeking more than $2 billion for its Kling AI video spinout at an $18 billion post-investment valuation, with General Atlantic in discussions to lead the round as the company eyes an IPO, Bloomberg reported June 17.

- Kling's ARR grew from $300 million in January to $500 million in March — driven by the February Kling 3.0 launch
- Target valuation was trimmed from $20 billion after investor pushback; negotiations ongoing
- On the same day, Kling released Kling 3.0 Turbo: faster video from ¥0.8/second at 720p with audio included, targeting high-volume ad and social production
- Kling AI: kling.ai — over 100 million registered users, 50,000 enterprise clients across 224 countries
- A Kling IPO would be the first dedicated AI video company to go public
blurb_zh:
据彭博社6月17日报道，快手正寻求为旗下Kling AI融资逾20亿美元，潜在领投方为General Atlantic，公司估值约180亿美元，并谋划IPO。

- Kling ARR从1月3亿美元增至3月5亿美元，增速由Kling 3.0的2月上线驱动
- 初始目标估值200亿美元，因投资者审慎态度已下调，谈判仍在进行中
- 同日，Kling发布3.0 Turbo版本：720p起含音频每秒0.8元，主打高产量内容的低成本快速生成
- Kling AI（kling.ai）：全球注册用户逾1亿，覆盖224个国家，企业客户5万家
verification_notes: Bloomberg broke the $2B/18B funding story June 17; confirmed by Foreign Policy Journal and other secondary sources. Turbo launch same day confirmed via Imagine.art, Atlas Cloud Blog, Morphic (secondary sources only; no official Kuaishou IR press release found for Turbo specifically). ARR figures from same Bloomberg article.

headline_zh: Signal总裁：AI聊天机器人不是你的朋友
blurb_en:
Signal President Meredith Whittaker said in a June 20 interview that treating AI chatbots as personal confidants is a structural mistake — especially as they gain agentic access to email, contacts, and calendars.

- 'When you click Allow, you're not just letting it read one email; you're opening a data pipeline,' she said, pointing to Microsoft Copilot's enterprise email access
- Signal's privacy model works because Signal never holds your messages; AI assistants are architecturally opposite — they need your data to function
- Her concern: the current market default is 'over-permissioned' agents, not AI itself
- Advice: audit AI agent permissions, prefer local models for sensitive use cases, avoid sharing medical or financial details with cloud-connected chatbots
- The warning lands as Google Gemini Spark promises to read your email 24/7 as an ambient agent
blurb_zh:
Signal总裁Meredith Whittaker在6月20日的采访中表示，将AI聊天机器人当作知心好友是一个架构层面的错误，尤其是在其获得读取邮件、通讯录和日历权限的当下。

- 她指出微软Copilot企业版可读取用户所有邮件，强调点击"允许"就是打开了一条数据管道
- Signal有效保护隐私，是因为平台从不持有用户数据；AI助手则恰恰相反
- 她的建议：审查AI代理权限，敏感场景优先使用本地模型，避免向云端AI分享医疗或财务信息
verification_notes: TechCrunch June 20 confirmed via site:techcrunch.com search. Windows News also covered the story. Whittaker's role as Signal President is well established. Copilot data pipeline claims consistent with Microsoft enterprise product documentation.
